agents.name: scout
agents.label: Repo Scout
agents.description: Cheap read-only subagent that maps relevant files, symbols, and code flow for the main model
promptGuidelines: Use scout automatically when you need to inspect unfamiliar code, map relevant files, or gather repo context before editing.
Use scout instead of spending expensive main-model turns on broad file discovery.
Do not use scout for trivial single-file reads or when the relevant files are already known.

agents.name: review
agents.label: Diff Review
agents.description: Cheap read-only subagent that reviews changed code for correctness bugs and regressions
promptGuidelines: Use review automatically after non-trivial edits or when reviewing staged/branch changes.
Use review for correctness, regression, edge-case, and maintainability review; avoid style-only comments.
Do not use review for tiny one-line edits unless high risk.

agents.name: tests
agents.label: Test Strategist
agents.description: Cheap read-only subagent that finds missing tests and high-risk cases
promptGuidelines: Use tests automatically after adding or changing behavior to identify missing high-value tests.
Use tests before writing tests when framework, fixtures, or existing test style are unknown.
Do not use tests for documentation-only changes.

agents.name: security
agents.label: Security Review
agents.description: Cheap read-only subagent that checks code for exploitable security issues
promptGuidelines: Use security automatically for auth, permissions, input parsing, file/network/process execution, secrets, payments, or data exposure changes.
Use security to trace sources to sinks and report exploitable issues only.
Do not use security for purely visual or copy changes.
